objective: Enhance the LLLO Curriculum Viewer (content-ko/tools/dict_viewer/viewer.html) to support interactive dictionary popups on word click (matching Flutter app behavior) and to display both old (production) and new (LLLO) course formats side by side.

context.current_state: The dict_viewer has atom-level word clicking via Vue.js, but only shows a placeholder 'Definition would appear here'. The lllo/tools/viewer.html has no atom support at all. Old production courses (lingo-frontend-web/assets/content/9_production/) are not loadable in either viewer.

new_lllo.atom_format: Array of {id, text, pos, upos} per line ID

old_production.structure: {id, type, pair, title, content: [{id, role, text, translation}]}

flutter_app_dictionary_layers: Layer 2: DictionaryResolver.getMeaning(id, locale) → translated meaning from language packs
Layer 3: DictionaryResolver.getSenses(id, locale) → multi-sense list with descriptions
Layer 4: DictionaryContent Word Breakdown → component decomposition via chunk mapping
Layer 6: LingoAtom.senseIndex → which sense to prioritize

tasks.title: Dictionary popup on word click (multi-layer)
tasks.goal: When user clicks a Korean word (atom) in the dialogue panel, show a popup/side panel with: surface text, POS badge, translated meaning, and atom breakdown — matching what the Flutter DictionaryContent widget does.
tasks.context: The dict_viewer already has selectAtom() and atom clicking. The placeholder panel at line 367-387 needs to be replaced with real dictionary resolution. Dictionary data is available in mapping.json (atom IDs) and the production i18n packs.
actions: A1: Load mapping.json (from content-ko/content/source/ko/i18n/) into the viewer at startup as a JS object. This maps surface forms → atom ID strings.
A2: Load production dictionary data (lingo-frontend-web/assets/content/9_production/packages/ko/i18n/dict_ko_zh_tw.json and dict_ko_en.json) as language packs, keyed by atom ID.
A3: Load dictionary_core.json (lingo-frontend-web/assets/content/9_production/packages/ko/core/dictionary_core.json) for base POS and sense data.

A4: Replace the placeholder 'Atom Details' panel with a real dictionary popup that shows: (a) Surface text (large), (b) POS badge (noun/verb/adj/etc from dictionary_core or atom ID prefix ko:n:/ko:v:/ko:adj:), (c) Translated meaning (from language pack for current targetLang), (d) Description/example if available in senses.

A5: Add Word Breakdown section: when an atom is part of a compound (has '+' in its ID), show each sub-atom as a mini card with its own POS and meaning, similar to CompositeId handling in DictionaryParser.
A6: Style the popup cleanly — no need for premium design, just functional: white background, rounded corners, POS as colored badge, meaning in grey text.
acceptance: Clicking any atom shows POS + meaning (not a placeholder).
Composite IDs (e.g. ko:n:경찰관+ko:p:로) show breakdown cards.
Switching targetLang between zh-TW and en-US changes the displayed meaning.
Works with the existing content.js data source.

tasks.title: Load and display old production courses
tasks.goal: The viewer should be able to display old production course content (from lingo-frontend-web/assets/content/9_production/) alongside the new LLLO curriculum. Currently the viewer only loads from content.js (new format).
tasks.context: Old courses use a flat JSON format: {id, type, pair, title, content: [{id, role, text, translation}]}. They don't have grammar notes or atom data, but they DO have dictionary/i18n data. The manifest.json lists all available lessons.
actions: A1: Extend content_builder.py (lllo/tools/content_builder.py) to ALSO scan old production courses from lingo-frontend-web/assets/content/9_production/packages/{lang}/dialogue/*.json and emit them into content.js under a separate section (e.g. courses.ko._production or a 'Legacy' level group).
A2: Normalize old format to match the new structure: map 'role' → 'speaker', 'translation' → translations map, keep 'id' and 'text'. No grammar notes for legacy lessons.
A3: In the viewer sidebar, show legacy lessons grouped under a 'Production (Legacy)' level heading, clearly separate from A1/A2/B1 etc.
A4: For old production dialogues, display text + translation (no atom clicking since atoms aren't available). Show a subtle indicator that this is a legacy lesson.
A5: Update the content_builder.py relative path resolution to locate the production packages from the lllo repo root (via ../lingo-frontend-web/ or a configurable env var FRONTEND_ASSETS_PATH).
acceptance: Legacy KO lessons (ko_l0_travel_001_airport etc.) appear in sidebar under 'Production (Legacy)' group.
Clicking a legacy lesson shows dialogue with text + translation.
New LLLO lessons (A1/L01-L25) still work exactly as before.
content_builder.py can build content.js with both old and new courses via: python tools/content_builder.py

tasks.title: Sync dict_viewer back to lllo/tools
tasks.goal: Replace the simpler lllo/tools/viewer.html with the enhanced dict_viewer version so there is one canonical viewer.
actions: A1: Copy the final enhanced viewer.html from content-ko/tools/dict_viewer/ to lllo/tools/viewer.html, replacing the old version.
A2: Ensure relative paths to content.js still resolve correctly (../data/generated/content.js).
A3: Remove content-ko/tools/dict_viewer/ directory (viewer now lives solely in lllo/tools/).
A4: Update any README references to point to the new canonical location.
acceptance: lllo/tools/viewer.html has all dict_viewer features (atom clicking, dictionary popup, legacy course support).
Opening viewer.html in a browser loads content correctly.

notes: VIEWER-01 and VIEWER-02 can be done in parallel by different agents.
VIEWER-03 is a cleanup step after both are done.
The viewer is a static HTML file using Vue.js 3 CDN — no build step required.
Dictionary data is loaded via relative path <script> tags or dynamic fetch — no server needed.
Keep the viewer simple and functional. No premium design required.
The Flutter frontend (DictionaryContent, DictionaryResolver, DictionaryParser) serves as REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION — reuse the same resolution logic in JS, which significantly reduces complexity.

format_evolution.description: The viewer serves as a prototype for how the frontend data format will evolve. Format decisions made here should anticipate the production frontend changes.

current_production_format.dialogue: {id, type, pair, title, content: [{id, role, text, translation}]}
current_production_format.atoms: NOT embedded in production dialogue JSON — resolved at runtime by Flutter DictionaryResolver

new_lllo_format.atoms: Embedded per-line in atoms[locale][lineId] — built by content_builder.py from CSV
new_lllo_format.dictionary: NOT in content.js — needs to be loaded separately

anticipated_changes_for_frontend: 1. Atoms should eventually be embedded in production dialogue JSON (currently missing). The content-pipeline must output atom arrays per line, matching what content_builder.py does for the viewer.
2. mapping.json (surface→atomID list) should remain the CANONICAL source of truth, consumed by both viewer (JS) and Flutter (Dart DictionaryResolver).
3. dictionary_core.json may need a 'word_type' field (sino/native/loan) for all entries — the viewer should display this, and the Flutter DictionaryContent already reads it.
4. Multi-sense entries need senseIndex on atoms — viewer should pioneer this UI (show sense selector or highlight primary sense) before Flutter adopts it.
5. The split mapping_*.json files (nouns/verbs/adj/proper/misc) are for development convenience only — production should always consume the merged mapping.json.
6. contextMeaning per-atom should be testable in the viewer first (JSON overlay on top of dictionary meaning) before being integrated into the Yarn build pipeline.
format_evolution.design_principle: The viewer acts as a rapid-iteration testbed for dictionary UX. Any format change validated in the viewer should inform corresponding changes to content-pipeline output and frontend LingoAtom/DictionaryResolver consumption.

gemini_prompt_template: 請讀取 VIEWER_ENHANCEMENT_TASKS.json，執行任務 {TASK_ID}。工作對象在 content-ko (viewer) 和 lllo (content_builder) 資料夾。Flutter 前端 (lingo-frontend-web) 是參考實作，請參考 DictionaryContent widget 和 DictionaryResolver 的邏輯。完成後請在瀏覽器打開 viewer.html 確認功能正常。
